articles.title: Abnormal heatwave conditions to persist across Tamil Nadu till July 17; rainfall set to improve later this week - The Hindu
articles.full_text:
The weak Southwest monsoon circulation, along with El Niño-induced subsidence, is expected to sustain abnormal heatwave conditions over interior Tamil Nadu till Friday (July 17, 2026). The Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) has forecast an improvement in rainfall over the State from the second half of the week and ease the prevailing heatwave conditions.
On Wednesday, many places, particularly interior parts of Tamil Nadu, continued to sizzle as the mercury level climbed above 40 degree Celsius. While the mercury levels hit 41.6 degrees Celsius in Vellore, the highest in the State for the day, places like Madurai and Tiruttani too witnessed temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius.
Chennai sweltered under searing heat on Wednesday, as Nungambakkam recorded a maximum temperature of 39.4 degrees Celsius, the highest for July in the city since 2019. The weather station had previously registered a maximum temperature of 40.4 degrees Celsius on July 5, 2019.
Meenambakkam too recorded a high day temperature of 40.1 degrees Celsius as strong South-westerlies and dry weather prevailed. The city may have to brace for another hot day, as the maximum temperature is likely to hover around 38-39 degrees Celsius on Thursday.
Officials of the RMC said the weak monsoon circulation over the Southern Peninsular region and El Niño-related atmospheric conditions led to limited cloud formation and an unusual heat spell in July. The RMC has issued a heatwave warning for isolated places in 19 interior and northern districts, including Vellore, Kancheepuram, Erode, Salem and Madurai, on Thursday, as day temperatures may soar two to four degrees Celsius above normal. Seven districts, including Namakkal, Karur and Madurai, are likely to experience heatwave conditions on Friday. Coastal areas of the State are likely to experience hot and humid weather till Friday.
V.R. Durai, Head, Regional Weather Forecasting Centre, RMC, said the weak Somali Jet, a low-level wind stream that transports moisture from the Arabian Sea, is likely to strengthen gradually from Friday and revive monsoon conditions over the Southern Peninsular region.
A weather system likely over the northwest Bay of Bengal would also support monsoon revival. Though the revival may be a weak one, it would still improve cloud cover and rainfall in the State from Friday and help keep temperatures in check.
While Madurai has experienced soaring July temperatures, various other places experienced unusual heatwave conditions spread over three to four days, he added.
Published - July 16, 2026 12:36 am IST
This is a premium article, available exclusively to subscribers.
The Hindu delivers independent, credible journalism that explains events with depth, context, and perspective.
*This offer is only for first-time subscribers.

articles.title: Heatwave alert in TN - newstodaynet.com
articles.full_text:
Chennai, July 16:
With a severe heatwave forecast across Tamil Nadu, health experts and authorities have sounded an urgent call to safeguard vulnerable groups, particularly senior citizens, who are at higher risk of heat-related illnesses.
According to advisories issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), temperatures in several parts of the State are expected to remain significantly above normal over the coming days. The warning has prompted medical professionals to stress the importance of preventive care, especially for the elderly, who often have reduced tolerance to extreme heat.
“Senior citizens are more susceptible to dehydration, heat exhaustion and heatstroke because their bodies do not regulate temperature as efficiently,” said Dr. R. Kumar, a Chennai-based geriatric specialist. “Even a short exposure to high temperatures can lead to serious complications if precautions are not taken.”

articles.title: IMD Issues Heatwave Alert for 19 Tamil Nadu Districts, Chennai Sizzles - BizzBuzz
articles.full_text:
IMD Issues Heatwave Alert for 19 Tamil Nadu Districts, Chennai Sizzles
IMD issues a heatwave warning for 19 Tamil Nadu districts as Madurai hits 42.5°C and Chennai swelters at 39°C amid high humidity and rough coastal seas.
IMD issues a heatwave warning for 19 districts as temperatures rise across Tamil Nadu.
Tamil Nadu is in the grip of one of its hottest spells of the season, with the India Meteorological Department issuing a heatwave warning for 19 districts on Thursday as soaring temperatures combine with heavy humidity to push conditions into dangerous territory.
The districts under alert span a wide stretch of the state, including Madurai, Vellore, Tiruvannamalai, Kancheepuram, Krishnagiri, Dharmapuri, Salem, Erode, Tiruchy, and Dindigul, among others. Residents in these areas have been urged to steer clear of prolonged sun exposure, drink plenty of water, and take extra care during the afternoon hours when temperatures typically peak.
Just how hot has it gotten? On Tuesday, Madurai Airport recorded 42.5 degrees Celsius, the highest temperature logged anywhere in India that day. Madurai city itself wasn't far behind at 41.6 degrees, while Tiruttani hit 40.5, Vellore reached 40.4, and even Chennai's Nungambakkam station touched 39.3 degrees. Weather officials say more than a dozen locations across the state recorded temperatures above 38.8 degrees, with several districts, including Karur, Dindigul, and the Nilgiris, running more than 5 degrees above what's normal for this time of year.
No Quick Break in Sight
The IMD expects the heat to persist into Friday across Virudhunagar, Sivaganga, Madurai, Dindigul, Karur, Namakkal, and Erode, suggesting the interior districts won't get relief for at least another day. Forecasters say daytime temperatures across the state's interior are likely to stay 2 to 4 degrees above the seasonal average through July 16, before conditions gradually ease closer to normal after that.
Coastal areas aren't escaping the discomfort either, even where the numbers technically fall short of heatwave thresholds. The combination of high heat and thick humidity is expected to make conditions feel considerably harsher than the thermometer alone would suggest, particularly along Tamil Nadu's coastline through July 17.
Chennai's Sticky Stretch
In Chennai, the mercury is expected to climb to around 39 degrees Celsius on Thursday. The city is forecast to stay partly cloudy over the next couple of days, but that cloud cover won't do much to cut the discomfort, with humidity keeping things feeling oppressive, especially in the afternoon and early evening.
There's a small silver lining building in the background. A shift in the strength of the prevailing westerly winds is expected to bring some relief to parts of the state, and light rainfall is likely over the Western Ghats districts through Saturday. On Friday, one or two spots along the north coast could see moderate rain, with light showers possible elsewhere in the state. It won't be enough to break the heat everywhere, but it should offer a bit of breathing room in the areas that catch it.
Rough Seas Add to the Warnings
Beyond the heat itself, authorities have also flagged rough sea conditions along the state's southern coast. Strong winds are expected over the Gulf of Mannar, the Comorin Sea, and nearby coastal waters, prompting the Regional Meteorological Centre to advise fishermen to stay ashore until conditions calm down.
Health officials are repeating the same core advice they give during every major heat spell: children, senior citizens, outdoor workers, and people with existing health conditions face the highest risk and should limit time outdoors during peak heat hours. Staying hydrated and avoiding direct sun exposure in the afternoon remain the simplest ways to stay safe until this spell of extreme heat finally breaks.

articles.title: Chennai Latest News Today on July 16th, 2026: CM Cabinet Meet, PM Projects, Blast & Heatwave Alert - LatestLY
articles.full_text:
Chennai Latest News Today on July 16th, 2026: CM Cabinet Meet, PM Projects, Blast & Heatwave Alert
Chennai sees CM's cabinet meeting for budget, PM Modi's project inaugurations, and a furnace blast. Heatwave alert issued as property tax deadline nears.
Chennai, July 16: Chennai is buzzing with significant political and developmental activities today, July 16th, as Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay chairs a crucial cabinet meeting to discuss the upcoming state budget and industrial projects. Prime Minister Narendra Modi is also in Tamil Nadu to inaugurate and lay foundation stones for development initiatives worth over ₹5,650 crore. Meanwhile, the city and state are bracing for an extended heatwave, even as local authorities manage civic services and address public safety concerns following a tragic industrial accident.
Top Stories
CM Vijay Chairs Cabinet Meeting on Budget & Industrial Projects
Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay is chairing a crucial Cabinet meeting at the Secretariat in Chennai today, July 16th. The agenda includes deliberations on proposals for the 2026-27 State Budget and the potential approval of new industrial projects for Tamil Nadu. Mumbai Latest News Today on July 16th, 2026: HC on Cleanliness, Navi Mumbai Airport & Railway Line Restored.
PM Modi to Inaugurate ₹5,650 Crore Development Projects in Tamil Nadu
Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to inaugurate and lay foundation stones for development projects worth ₹5,650 crore across Tamil Nadu today, July 16th. These initiatives are expected to boost infrastructure and economic growth in the state.
One Killed, Seven Injured in Gummidipoondi SIPCOT Furnace Blast
A tragic furnace blast occurred at the SIPCOT Industrial Estate in Gummidipoondi, near Chennai, on July 15th. The incident resulted in the death of one guest worker and injuries to seven others, prompting the state government to announce compensation for the victims. Delhi Latest News Today on July 16th, 2026: Red Fort Closed, Officials Fined & Sonam Wangchuk's Health.
CM Vijay Stakes Claim to Form Government
Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has reportedly staked a claim to form the government in Tamil Nadu, securing support from the Congress, VCK, IUML, and Left parties. This significant political development was reported on July 15th.
Politics & Governance
CM Launches 'Nalam TN' Health Portal
The Chief Minister launched the 'Nalam TN' health portal on July 15th, marking a new health initiative for Tamil Nadu. This digital platform aims to enhance health services across the state, including Chennai.
IAS Officers Reshuffled Across Tamil Nadu
A significant reshuffle of IAS officers in Tamil Nadu was announced on July 15th, affecting several key positions. Notable transfers include J. Radhakrishnan and Supriya Sahu, among others.
Madras High Court to Hear PIL for CBI Probe into TVK MLA Bribery Case
A public interest litigation petition seeking a CBI probe into allegations that TVK MLA N. Elayaraja was offered a ₹35 crore bribe is listed for hearing before the first Division Bench of the Madras High Court today, July 16th.
Tamil Nadu Withdraws Supreme Court Appeal in Armstrong Murder Case
Tamil Nadu has withdrawn its appeal in the Supreme Court against a CBI probe into the Armstrong murder case. This decision was reported on July 15th, indicating a shift in the state's legal strategy.
Civic & Urban Development
GCC Extends Property Tax Collection Camps Until July 31st
The Greater Chennai Corporation (GCC) has extended its special property tax collection camps until July 31st. This extension comes as nearly half of Chennai's property owners have yet to pay their dues, with the civic body aiming to recover essential revenue.
Sewage Pumping Halted in Teynampet and Adyar Today
The Chennai Metropolitan Water Supply and Sewerage Board announced on July 15th that sewage pumping operations will be suspended in the Teynampet and Adyar areas today, July 16th. Residents in these localities should anticipate temporary disruptions to services.
PM Modi to Inaugurate Revamped Chennai Park Railway Station Tomorrow
Prime Minister Modi is scheduled to inaugurate the upgraded Chennai Park railway station tomorrow, July 17th. The station underwent renovation at a cost of Rs 14.79 crore as part of the Centre's Amrit Station Scheme.
Public Consultation Held for Mamallan Reservoir Project Amid Opposition
A public consultation meeting was held on July 14th for the proposed Mamallan Reservoir project near Tiruporur in Chengalpattu. Designed to store 1.65 TMC of rainwater, the project faces opposition from local fishermen concerned about their livelihoods and potential disruption to marine species.
Crime & Public Safety
Man Arrested for Murder and Gold Theft to Fund Wife's Hospital Costs
Chennai police arrested a man on July 15th who allegedly murdered a woman and stole 12 sovereigns of gold. Investigations revealed the accused reportedly committed the crime to cover his pregnant wife's hospital expenses.
Woman Killed Shielding Daughter from Jilted Fiancé's Attack
A tragic incident occurred on July 15th where a woman was reportedly killed while shielding her daughter from an attack by a jilted fiancé wielding a sickle. The incident highlights concerns about public safety in the region.
Operation Toofan: Tamil Nadu and Kerala Join Hands for Anti-Drug Drive
Tamil Nadu and Kerala are collaborating on "Operation Toofan," an anti-drug drive. Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay met with Chennithala on July 15th to discuss the initiative and is also set to join actor Mohanlal for an anti-drug awareness event.
Health & Education
Tamil Nadu Adds Over 1000 MBBS Seats
Tamil Nadu has added over 1000 MBBS seats in government medical colleges, bringing the total number of seats available across 36 institutions to 5,200. This expansion, announced on July 15th by Health Minister Arunraj, aims to boost medical education capacity in the state.
Economy, Environment & Natural Phenomena
Chennai Flags Off First India-UK CETA Commercial Consignments
The first commercial consignments, valued at $446,046, were officially flagged off from Chennai on July 15th. This marks the implementation of the India-United Kingdom Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), with shipments including gold jewellery, automotive components, and leather footwear.
Madras High Court to Examine Construction Ban Around Pallikaranai Marshland
The Madras High Court is scheduled to examine the National Green Tribunal's (NGT) construction ban within a 1 km radius around the Pallikaranai marshland on August 3rd. This review follows an order issued on July 15th, addressing environmental concerns in the area.
ICAR-CIBA Chennai Successfully Demonstrates Blue Swimmer Crab Farming
ICAR-CIBA in Chennai has successfully demonstrated India's first blue swimmer crab farming technique. The achievement, announced on July 15th, utilized hatchery-produced seeds and developed formulated feed, showcasing advancements in aquaculture.
3-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Krishnagiri, Tremors Felt in Karnataka
A 3-magnitude earthquake struck Krishnagiri in Tamil Nadu on July 15th, with tremors reportedly felt in parts of neighboring Karnataka. There were no immediate reports of significant damage or casualties.
Photographers Document Lives of Palm Workers in Tamil Nadu
Young photographers from Tamil Nadu are documenting the hidden lives of palm workers across the state. This initiative, highlighted on July 15th, aims to bring attention to the challenges and contributions of this community.
Culture & Sports
TTDC Launches 'Know Chennai' Heritage Tour for Students
The Tamil Nadu Tourism Development Corporation (TTDC) launched the 'Know Chennai' heritage tour for school students on July 15th. This initiative aims to educate young minds about the city's rich history and cultural landmarks.
World Champion D Gukesh to Face Nihal Sarin at Chennai Grandmasters
World Champion D. Gukesh is set to compete against Nihal Sarin in the first round of the Chennai Grandmasters chess tournament. This highly anticipated match was announced on July 15th, drawing attention from the chess community.
Weather & Outlook
Heatwave 'Orange Alert' Issued for Chennai Amid Light Drizzle, High Near 38°C
An 'Orange Alert' for heatwave conditions has been extended for Chennai and 19 districts across Tamil Nadu for today, July 16th, and tomorrow, July 17th. While light drizzle and overcast skies are expected in Chennai, residents are advised to take precautions against the intense heat, with temperatures expected to range between 29°C and 38°C.
Weather: Overcast — 32.5°C. Today: Light drizzle, 29°C – 38°C.
Chennai Weather Live Tracker Map on Windy
As Chennai navigates these key developments, residents are urged to stay informed on public health and civic announcements. The outcomes of today's high-level meetings are expected to shape the state's economic and social landscape in the coming months, while environmental and cultural initiatives continue to foster community engagement and preservation.
(The above story first appeared on LatestLY on Jul 16, 2026 08:59 AM IST. For more news and updates on politics, world, sports, entertainment and lifestyle, log on to our website latestly.com).

articles.title: வெப்ப அலை தொடர்பான முன்னெச்சரிக்கை நடவடிக்கைகள்: தலைமை ஆசிரியர்களுக்கு உத்தரவு - Hindu Tamil Thisai
articles.search_term: ("வெப்ப அலை" OR "அக்னி நட்சத்திரம்" OR "கடும் வெயில்" OR "கொளுத்தும் வெயில்" OR "எரிக்கும் வெயில்" OR "கடுமையான வெப்பம்" OR "வெயில் கொடுமை" OR "ஹீட் வேவ்" OR "கொடும் வெயில்" OR "வெப்பத்தின் தாக்கம்" OR "அசாதாரண வெப்பம்") Tamil Nadu
articles.full_text:
சென்னை: பள்ளிக்கல்வித்துறை இயக்குநர் ச.கண்ணப்பன், அனைத்து மாவட்ட முதன்மைக் கல்வி அலுவலர்களுக்கும் அனுப்பிய சுற்றறிக்கை விவரம்:
தமிழகத்தில் நிலவும் வெப்ப அலை மற்றும் அதிக வெப்பநிலைச் சூழல்களால் மாணவர்களின் உடல்நலன் பாதிக்கப்பட வாய்ப்புள்ளது. எனவே, பள்ளி மாணவர்களின் நலனை உறுதிசெய்ய அறிவுறுத்தல்கள் வழங்கப்படுகின்றன.
இதுதவிர இந்திய வானிலை ஆய்வு மையத்தால் கடுமையான வெப்ப அலை என முன்கூட்டியே தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டால், மாணவர்களின் பாதுகாப்பு மற்றும் நலனை முன்னிறுத்தி தேவையான அனைத்து முன்னெச்சரிக்கை நடவடிக்கைகளையும் எடுக்க வேண்டும்.
மாவட்ட நிர்வாகத்தின் வழிகாட்டுதல்களின்படி பள்ளிகளுக்கு விடுமுறை அறிவித்தல் உள்ளிட்ட நடவடிக்கைகளை மேற்கொள்ள தலைமையாசிரியர்கள் தயார்நிலையில் இருக்க வேண்டும்.
மாணவர்களுக்கு போதிய குடிநீர் வசதி, நிழலான ஓய்விட வசதி உட்பட வெப்ப அலைக்கான பாதுகாப்பு அம்சங்கள் முறையாக பின்பற்றப்படுவதை அனைத்து முதன்மைக் கல்வி அலுவலர்களும் உறுதிசெய்ய வேண்டும்.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Weather: தமிழகத்தில் இரு நாள்கள் வெப்ப அலை எச்சரிக்கை.. எப்போது மழை?.. வானிலை அப்டேட் இதோ - Times Now Tamil
articles.search_term: (எச்சரிக்கை OR நிவாரணம் OR "சிவப்பு எச்சரிக்கை" OR "ரெட் அலர்ட்" OR "ஆரஞ்சு எச்சரிக்கை" OR "மஞ்சள் எச்சரிக்கை" OR "நிவாரண நடவடிக்கை" OR "ஹீட் ஆக்சன் பிளான்") Tamil Nadu
articles.full_text:
தமிழகத்தில் இந்த ஜூலை மாதத்தில் வழக்கத்திற்கு மாறாக கடும் தீவிர வெப்ப அலை வீசி வருகிறது. குறிப்பாக கடந்த சில நாள்களாகவே பகல் நேர வெயில் உச்சம் தொட்டு மக்களை வாட்டி வதைக்கிறது. அதன்படி, நேற்றைய தினம் தமிழகத்தில் சென்னை, திருவள்ளூர், வேலூர், ஈரோடு, கரூர், நாகை, திருச்சி, நெல்லை ஆகிய மாவட்டங்களில் வெயில் சதமடித்தது.
நேற்று அதிகபட்சமாக வேலூரில் மாவட்டத்தில் 107 டிகிரி பாரன்ஹீட் வெப்பம் பதிவானது. அத்துடன் இயல்பை விட பல பகுதிகளில் வெப்பநிலை 2-4 டிகிரி அதிகரித்து காணப்பட்டது.
இந்நிலையில், தமிழகத்தில் வெயிலின் தாக்கம் மற்றும் மழையின் நிலவரம் என்ன என இன்றைய (ஜூலை 16) வானிலை நிலவரம் குறித்த முக்கிய தகவல்களை வானிலை ஆய்வு மையம் வழங்கியுள்ளது.
தமிழ்நாடு வானிலை நிலவரம்
தமிழக வானிலை நிலவரம் (Tamil Nadu Weather) குறித்து சென்னை வானிலை ஆய்வு மையம் வெளியிட்டுள்ள அறிக்கையில் கூறியுள்ளதாவது, மேற்கு திசை காற்றின் வேகமாறுபாடு காரணமாக, இன்று மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.நாளை (ஜூலை 17) அன்று மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்கள், வடக்கு கடலோர தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி, மின்னலுடன் கூடிய மழைக்கு வாய்ப்புள்ளது. அத்துடன் தமிழகத்தில் ஜூலை 21ம் தேதி வரை மழை தொடரும்.
ஜூலை 18 அன்று மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.
இன்றைய தினம் திருப்பத்தூர், வேலூர், ராணிப்பேட்டை, கள்ளக்குறிச்சி, திருவண்ணாமலை, காஞ்சிபுரம், கிருஷ்ணகிரி, தருமபுரி, சேலம், நாமக்கல், கரூர், ஈரோடு, திருச்சி, அரியலூர், பெரம்பலூர், சிவகங்கை, விருதுநகர், திண்டுக்கல் மற்றும் மதுரை மாவட்டங்களில் வெப்ப அலை வீசக்கூடும்.
நாளை (ஜூலை 17) விருதுநகர், சிவகங்கை, மதுரை, திண்டுக்கல், கரூர், நாமக்கல், ஈரோடு ஆகிய மாவட்டங்களில் வெப்ப அலை வீசக்கூடும் என எச்சரிக்கை தரப்பட்டுள்ளது.
சென்னை வானிலை (Chennai Weather)
சென்னை மற்றும் புறநகர் பகுதிகளை பொருத்தவரை இன்று ஓரளவு மேகமூட்டத்துடன் காணப்படும். அதிகபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 38-39 டிகிரி செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும், குறைந்தபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 28-29 டிகிரி செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும் இருக்கக்கூடும்.மீனவர்களுக்கு எச்சரிக்கை
இன்று தமிழக கடலோரப்பகுதிகள், மன்னார் வளைகுடா மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய குமரிக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 45 முதல் 55 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 65 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் வீசக்கூடும்.தென்மேற்கு வங்கக்கடலின் சில பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 45 முதல் 55 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 65 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும், அந்தமான் கடல் பகுதிகள், வடக்கு வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகளை ஒட்டிய மத்திய வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 60 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் வீசக்கூடும். எனவே, மேற்குறிப்பிட்ட நாள்களில் மீனவர்கள் சம்பந்தப்பட்ட பகுதிகளுக்கு செல்ல வேண்டாம் என எச்சரிக்கை தரப்பட்டுள்ளது.
